content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the backend-exhaustion changes in `process_backend`, `completion`, `final_review`, `prompt_review`, `engine`, `retry_policy`, and the related tests. `cargo test --quiet` passes. I found one remaining correctness gap: the OpenRouter adapter only treats 429 exhaustion responses as terminal, so quota/billing exhaustion returned under other 4xx statuses will still retry instead of degrading.

## Amendments

1. `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:205-220` and `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:345-377` only upgrade exhaustion to `FailureClass::BackendExhausted` for HTTP 429. Any credit/billing exhaustion that comes back as another 4xx (for example 402/403 with quota text in the body) still falls through to retryable `TransportFailure`/generic `BackendUnavailable`, so the run will keep retrying instead of degrading gracefully. The body-pattern check should run before the status-specific split, or at least cover the other billing/quota statuses in both `send_probe` and `map_http_error`.
   Rationale: The feature goal is graceful degradation on persistent backend exhaustion. Leaving non-429 quota/billing responses retryable means one class of real exhaustion failures is still handled incorrectly for OpenRouter-backed panels.
